HomePage.hero_subtitle: A Franco-American platform documenting real human stories from death row
HomePage.title: Giving visibility and dignity to the unseen.
HomePage.mission: Voices On Death Row is a Franco-American advocacy platform dedicated to giving visibility and dignity to people sentenced to death. Through letters, interviews, art, and testimonies, we document real human stories that are often erased or misunderstood. Our goal is to humanize, educate, and spark meaningful conversations about the realities of the American justice system.
HomePage.archive_desc: A trustworthy space for letters, profiles, and creative works preserved for the public, researchers, and families.
HomePage.podcast_desc: Listen to first-hand accounts from those sentenced to death, speaking in their own words.
HomePage.contact_desc: We welcome correspondence from researchers, journalists, families, and supporters. Inquire about contributions or participation.

IntroSequence.title: Voices On Death Row
IntroSequence.subtitle: Stories, letters, and reflections from behind prison walls.

VoicesPage.title: About the Project
VoicesPage.description: Voices On Death Row was born from 17 years of correspondence and has grown into a comprehensive advocacy platform combining storytelling, education, and archival preservation. The project is rooted in a deep commitment to humanizing those within the justice system and preserving their stories for the public record.

StoriesPage.title: Profiles of the Incarcerated
StoriesPage.description: Documenting real human stories that are often erased or misunderstood. This section is dedicated to preserving the identity and dignity of those within the system.

ArchivePage.title: Letters & Writings Archive
ArchivePage.description: A trustworthy archive of correspondence, documenting the lived experiences of death row inmates through their own words.

ArtPage.title: Art & Creative Works
ArtPage.description: Giving visibility to the humanity and creativity of incarcerated individuals through their visual and creative expressions.

PodcastPage.title: Audio Testimonies
PodcastPage.description: Preserving voices for the public, researchers, and families. Listen to the first-hand accounts of those sentenced to death.

AboutPage.title: About the Project
AboutPage.description: Voices On Death Row is a Franco-American advocacy platform dedicated to giving visibility and dignity to people sentenced to death. Founded by Halima Kilgore and inspired by 17 years of correspondence with Borgela, a Haitian man sentenced to death in Pennsylvania, this platform documents real human stories that are often erased or misunderstood. Through letters, interviews, art, and testimonies, we aim to raise awareness, humanize those within the justice system, and spark meaningful conversations about the realities of capital punishment in America.

ResourcesPage.title: Educational Resources
ResourcesPage.description: Information and research materials regarding the justice system, human rights, and the reality of the death penalty.

ContactPage.title: Contact & Participation
ContactPage.description: We welcome correspondence from researchers, journalists, and families. Use the form below or reach out via email.
ContactPage.form_description: We welcome correspondence from researchers, journalists, and families. If you are interested in contributing to the archive or participating in the podcast, please reach out.

Footer.about_desc: Voices On Death Row is a Franco-American advocacy platform founded by Halima Kilgore and inspired by 17 years of correspondence with Borgela, a Haitian man sentenced to death in Pennsylvania. We are dedicated to giving visibility and dignity to people sentenced to death. Through letters, interviews, art, and testimonies, we document real human stories that are often erased or misunderstood, aiming to humanize, educate, and spark meaningful conversations about the justice system.
